title: Signal-flow note example
html:
A second note so the archive does not feel empty.

Small systems note

Consider the one-pole low-pass response

$$ H(s) = \frac{1}{1 + s/\omega_c}. $$

For \(\omega \ll \omega_c\), the gain is approximately \(1\). For \(\omega \gg \omega_c\), the magnitude decays like \(1/\omega\).

Warning
Swap this note out with one of your own as soon as you start using the site.
